channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Antigravity is a Google Cloud tool for automating file system workflows and repository organization using timeboxes and tags. It helps developers reduce time spent searching for files and projects, improving development efficiency.

item.title: GPT 5.6 is out… but not for you lol
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej comments that GPT 5.6 has been released but is not widely available to general users. The video title uses a dismissive tone to highlight the limited access to the latest model version.

item.title: Use These 17 Claude Plugins, It Will Make You 10x Better.
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI presents 17 Claude plugins, CLIs, and skills organized by category: design, productivity, and data. The tools include web scraping (Firecrawl), RAG systems (LightRAG), automation (Playwright CLI), and integrations with GitHub, Stripe, and Supabase, intended to enhance Claude Code workflow efficiency.

item.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
item.description: AI Engineer: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for brand voice in AI systems: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach ensures brand voice consistency across interactions and prevents prompt degradation over extended conversations.

item.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer about systematic patterns underlying Renaissance trials. The discussion explores hidden institutional and procedural systems that governed how trials operated during the Renaissance period.

item.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS Developers presents a 7-step system design framework prioritizing problem understanding before technology selection. The framework covers requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints, using a URL shortener as the example interview problem.

item.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: An industry summit from June 2026 featuring leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and others discussing the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, the AI infrastructure buildout, enterprise AI budgets, cybersecurity implications, and the competitive compute race. Key themes include whether the recall constitutes an effective AI pause, concerns about China's advantage, the viability of announced data centers, and shifts from token optimization to outcome optimization.

item.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel discusses the next generation of AI training paradigms beyond standard supervised learning. The essay explores concepts like grindability, verifiability, reinforcement learning with verification (RLVR), test-time scaling, and dreaming as potential directions for future model training.

item.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
item.description: Better Stack: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents with support for streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to enable natural conversational interactions where agents can be interrupted and handle edge cases that typical STT-LLM-TTS pipelines fail to address.

item.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Muhammad Farooq from Google Cloud presents five critical components required in an AI agent harness beyond the model itself. He argues that the harness is what translates model predictions into functional deployed agents, distinguishing production-ready systems from demonstrations.

item.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
item.description: AI Master: Gemini Canvas is a Google AI tool that builds websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from prompts within Gemini. The tutorial demonstrates six levels of functionality from document editing to advanced combinations.

item.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
item.description: CS50: A CS50 video featuring Professor David J. Malan discussing how he discovered computer science as his field. CS50 is Harvard University's foundational course in computer science and programming available across multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts thousands of personal notes and documents into queryable memory for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, index, wiki) with a file-based deep research algorithm that avoids traditional vector or graph databases, enabling efficient context loading for language models.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a new family of open agentic coding models from DeepReinforce available in sizes from 9B to 397B parameters, built on Gemma and Qwen foundations. The models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with MIT licensing and GGUF versions for local deployment. The 35B variant shows strong practical performance for local agent applications.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: A video tutorial shows how to generate a professional one-pager marketing document from a single text prompt using Venngage AI. The workflow covers content generation, design, copy editing, visual asset selection, and PDF export, completing the full process in 15 minutes.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa presents experience building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The session covers core components including agent loops, A2A protocol, Effect-TS implementation, evals and feedback systems, long context handling, monitoring, tool building, and enterprise patterns using Claude and Cursor.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context awareness and memory, using the metaphor of a brilliant engineer who can only see fragments of code and forgets between interactions. He positions this as a fundamental problem to solve in agent design.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: The video claims that Fable 5, a Claude model previously withdrawn over national security concerns, may be returning based on leaked code strings, AWS Bedrock listing, and Congressional pressure. It argues that Anthropic is advancing faster and may release the model with government scrutiny and a stronger Mythos successor waiting.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and continuity should be centered on durable session logs (the data layer) rather than models, frameworks, or tools. As models commoditize, the differentiator shifts to persistent logs that enable agent portability, durability, and state management across different compute, runtime, and model providers.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Recursive Language Models represent an inference-time compute paradigm that can be applied to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The presentation maps RLM principles onto agent workflows and explores how Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse guide agents to recursively solve complex tasks with declarable outcomes.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Ada Palmer discusses the historical and social role of forgiveness, potentially exploring how it functioned as a constraint or mechanism in past societies. This is a philosophical or historical commentary piece.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: AI Master presents an analysis of GTA VI's actual AI architecture, debunking viral "GTA VI + GPT-6" claims by examining Rockstar patents, job postings, and leaked design docs. The video identifies Rockstar's real AI stack as focused on real-time behavioral simulation, persistent NPC memory, and dynamic systems rather than relying on OpenAI models.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented new data analytics agents with enhanced capabilities for data engineering, data science, and conversational analytics. The session covered the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations accessible through Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents elevate software engineering from line-by-line coding to systems-level design. Engineering discipline remains essential through systems thinking, decomposition, and state management, repositioning developer value in an AI-native world.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Fable is a frontier AI model developed by Anthropic's Labs division, led by Mike Krieger. The model was pulled from the market following government action during the period discussed in this podcast interview.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AI-native databases are presented as essential infrastructure for powering agents with enterprise data. The talk advocates for multimodal querying, graph retrieval-augmented generation, and adoption of open standards like Model Context Protocol to enable high-fidelity context and rapid agent deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Spanner now supports hybrid search combining full-text and vector search for AI-powered applications. The feature integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to enable retrieval-augmented generation and contextual search at scale.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) provide standardized integration for agents to connect to data sources including BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB without custom connectors. Agent operations (AgentOps) features enable observability into performance and cost with minimal instrumentation.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry as a platform for building production agents that can access enterprise data systems. The session covered integrating with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, both existing and custom-built, to connect agents with tools and other agents across SaaS and on-premises systems.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind announces upgrades to Gemini including improved model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The update emphasizes support for agentic applications in Vertex AI and advances in agent design research.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud offers a low-code approach for small teams to build AI applications without AI expertise by combining Gemini, Cloud SQL, and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL. The platform auto-generates code and enables advanced capabilities such as natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendations.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements to Looker that integrate Gemini AI and dashboard agents to automate business intelligence workflows. The update emphasizes a governed semantic layer for grounding AI in trusted data, with new features including conversational analytics and pre-trained agents for scaling expertise.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in NoSQL databases across Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable at Google Cloud Next 2026, emphasizing architectural innovations and product roadmaps optimized for AI and agentic applications. The session covered latest releases and customer implementations of these high-performance, scalable database services.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind has developed a Deep research API that powers agents built on Gemini to complete comprehensive research tasks with improved performance. The API is available through Google Cloud for developers to integrate these capabilities into their applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Run introduces new serverless features designed to simplify deployment of AI agents and inference workloads. The updates emphasize seamless scaling and reduced operational overhead for next-generation AI-driven applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new features in Cloud SQL supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server with AI-driven capabilities for automated health management and fleet-wide insights. The update emphasizes performance optimization, high availability, and security improvements for mission-critical applications.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced upgrades to its agent platform including Agent Builder with no-code/low-code Designer, Agent Registry for reusable components, unified governance with time-travel debugging, and an Agent Development Kit. The platform provides enterprise-grade tools for building, scaling, and managing multi-agent workflows.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas identifies Miranda distortion as a structural failure mode in persona-based AI systems where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing models to optimize for convincingness rather than accuracy. Current evaluation benchmarks like InCharacter-style metrics fail to detect anachronistic reasoning because they measure fluency and coherence instead of epistemic fidelity. Thomas proposes a shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation with

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, taught by David J. Malan. It is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare, with accompanying communities on Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and other social platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source coding-focused agentic model with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture, one trillion parameters, and 256K context window. It includes a CLI tool for repository exploration, code editing, and testing, plus a plan mode for proposing changes before execution.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a major in-person AI engineering conference held June 30, 2026 in San Francisco at Moscone West. Day 1 programming includes keynotes and main-stage sessions focused on software factories and AI engineering topics.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI presents an agentic OS setup framed as a way to improve Claude Code productivity and outcomes. The approach covers skills, loop engineering, and memory management as core components that enhance both custom projects and shareable team packages.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.
